tier_intro: This is where your AI agent gets smart. The more you give it here, the less it has to guess — and the fewer times it'll say 'I'm not sure, let me check.' This tier is primarily file-based. Upload what you have.

tier_complete.headline: Knowledge base loaded.
tier_complete.body: Your AI agent now has access to your FAQs, policies, procedures, and known issues. It can answer the real questions your customers actually ask — accurately and instantly. Next: tell it about your competitive landscape.

questions.question: Upload your FAQ document — or any document with common customer questions and answers.
questions.placeholder: FAQ, help center export, knowledge base dump, support playbook — anything with Q&A
questions.meta_profiling: Presence and quality of FAQ doc reveals support function maturity. Well-organized FAQ = systematic approach to customer education. Absent = reactive support culture.
questions.why_this_matters: A single FAQ upload can train AI to answer hundreds of questions accurately. This is the single highest-leverage action in the entire setup.

questions.question: No FAQ doc? Type your top 10 questions and answers here.
questions.placeholder:
Q: How long does delivery take?
A: Standard delivery is 3-5 business days. Express is next day if ordered before 2pm.

Q: Can I return a product?
A: Yes, within 30 days...
questions.meta_profiling: Manually written FAQs reveal what the business considers most important vs. what customers actually ask. Gaps between the two are worth flagging.
questions.why_this_matters: Even 10 well-written Q&As will dramatically reduce AI deflection rate and improve accuracy on the most common customer questions.

questions.question: Upload your policy documents — returns, shipping, privacy, terms of service, etc.
questions.placeholder: You can upload multiple files — returns policy, shipping policy, T&Cs, privacy policy
questions.meta_profiling: Policy completeness reveals legal and operational maturity. Missing privacy policy in a GDPR context = immediate risk flag.
questions.why_this_matters: Policy questions are among the highest-volume customer queries. AI that can answer them accurately, instantly, and consistently removes a huge support burden.

questions.question: Do you have Standard Operating Procedures or internal guides for customer-facing scenarios?
questions.text_placeholder: Or describe key procedures in plain language — e.g. how to handle a refund request, escalation path for a complaint, etc.
questions.meta_profiling: SOPs signal operational maturity. Detailed SOPs = AI can follow processes precisely. Absent = AI needs more judgment and a tighter escalation policy.
questions.why_this_matters: SOPs tell AI exactly how to handle edge cases — not just what to say, but what steps to take. This prevents AI from inventing its own process.

questions.question: Are there any current issues, service disruptions, or known problems customers should know about?
questions.placeholder: e.g. 'We're experiencing delays on orders placed after March 10 due to supplier issues', 'Feature X is temporarily unavailable', 'Known bug with checkout on Safari — workaround is...'
questions.meta_profiling: Willingness to document current issues reveals transparency culture. Companies that admit issues proactively have lower escalation rates.
questions.why_this_matters: Customers who are experiencing a known issue need acknowledgment immediately. AI that already knows about it can respond with empathy and a fix — instead of pretending it doesn't exist.

questions.question: What questions do customers ask that you DON'T have a good answer for yet?
questions.placeholder: This is valuable too — questions you're still figuring out. AI will flag these for human review rather than guessing.
questions.meta_profiling: Knowledge gaps reveal business blind spots. High-frequency gaps = product or process improvements needed. AI flags these for systematic resolution.
questions.why_this_matters: AI that knows what it doesn't know is far more trustworthy than one that confidently gives wrong answers. Knowledge gaps trigger 'escalate to human' behavior.

questions.question: How often does your knowledge base change?

options.label: Rarely — things stay pretty stable, maybe a few updates a year
Monthly — regular updates to products, policies, or pricing
Weekly — fast-moving business, things change often
Daily — inventory, pricing, or availability changes constantly
Seasonally — big changes at specific times of year

questions.meta_profiling: Update frequency determines how aggressively AI should flag potentially outdated information and how often knowledge base reviews should be scheduled.
questions.why_this_matters: Fast-changing businesses need AI that actively flags when its own knowledge might be stale. This setting triggers update reminders and staleness warnings.

questions.question: Where is the most up-to-date version of your information?

options.label: Our website — always up to date
A specific person on the team
Scattered — different systems for different topics

questions.meta_profiling: 'Person' answer = single point of failure, worth flagging. 'Scattered' = knowledge management problem that AI will partially solve but can't fully compensate for.
questions.why_this_matters: AI needs to know where to point for authoritative answers it can't give directly — and when knowledge may need verification before acting on it.

questions.question: When AI isn't certain about something, it should...

options.label: Clearly say it doesn't know and escalate to a human
Give the best answer it has but flag it as uncertain
Redirect to your website, docs, or help center
Ask a clarifying question first — maybe it misunderstood
Train it so thoroughly it should rarely be uncertain

questions.meta_profiling: 'Never uncertain' answer with a thin knowledge base = a calibration gap to address. Best response depends on the company's risk tolerance and support model.
questions.why_this_matters: This is the confidence calibration. An AI that confidently gives wrong answers is worse than one that admits uncertainty. This setting defines that threshold.

questions.question: Are there any URLs — help centers, product documentation, or public knowledge bases — we should include?
questions.meta_profiling: Structured online knowledge resources indicate investment in scalable support. Help centers with search = mature support operation.
questions.why_this_matters: AI can pull directly from your existing help center content — no re-entry needed. Also lets AI direct customers to self-service resources when appropriate.
